figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of ACP with RBAC and Zero Trust across agent governance criteria.
Table 13: ACR-1.0 sequence test vectors. All 5/5 pass in library mode with the Go reference implementation.

Figure 1: ACP end-to-end verifiability pipeline. The TLA+ model defines the invariants that test vectors instantiate and the compliance runner validates at runtime. The ACP engine records every decision in the append-only audit ledger. The TLA+ layer is a static verification artifact; it does not run at admission time.
Table 8: Go reference implementation packages (23 total). All pass go test ./....
Table 9: Conformance test vector suites. All positive vectors carry real Ed25519 signatures (RFC 8037 Test Key A) and real SHA-256 hash chains.

Table 10: ACP-RISK-2.0 engine benchmarks (go test -bench=. -benchtime=3s). Full evaluation with all three F_anom rules completes in under 1.4 µs. The cooldown short-circuit path (149 ns) avoids the risk function entirely. All measurements use InMemoryQuerier; production implementations with persistent storage will incur additional I/O latency.
Table 3: ACP-RISK-2.0 risk scoring factors (base formula; Fanom adds up to +30 from pattern accumulation).
Table 6: ACP prohibited behaviors. Exhibiting any disqualifies conformance at all levels.
Table 11: ACR-1.0 sequence vector results under strict mode (–strict).
Table 2: ACP formal agent tuple fields.
Table 4: STRIDE threat model and ACP mitigations.

Table 7: ACP v1.17 active specification documents by conformance level (38 primary documents shown). The remaining 3 documents are the ACP-D decentralized suite (L5, design phase) not yet published. New in v1.16: ACP-RISK-2.0 (L2, deterministic engine v2 with F_anom + Cooldown), ACP-SIGN-2.0 (L1, PQC hybrid spec). New in v1.17: ACR-1.0 sequence compliance runner, TLA+ formal model, ACP-SIGN-2.0 HYBRID stub (pkg/sign2/). Superseded versions (CONF1.0, CONF-1.1, LEDGER-1.2, REP-1.1, AGENT-SPEC-0.3, DCMA-1.0, CROSS-ORG-1.0, REPPORTABILITY-1.0) archived in archive/specs/.
